mimo_desc: Two hole-punch marks visible at top of page, flanking the "RESTRICTED" stamp.
gpt_desc: *No image content on this page.*
mini_desc: *Image: Scanned typewritten Air Force memorandum page titled “AIR INTELLIGENCE REQUIREMENTS MEMORANDUM NUMBER 4” and “UNCONVENTIONAL AIRCRAFT,” dated 15 February 1949, with “RESTRICTED” at top and bottom, plus handwritten and stamped archive markings near the lower margin.*

elements.description: Typed memorandum document from the Department of the Air Force, Directorate of Intelligence, dated 15 February 1949. Subject: 'AIR INTELLIGENCE REQUIREMENTS MEMORANDUM NUMBER 4 - UNCONVENTIONAL AIRCRAFT'. Contains typed text outlining purpose, rescission, and reporting procedures. Includes 'RESTRICTED' headers, handwritten annotations ('additional copies behind file'), a red stamp ('ENCL 62-83894-164'), and a form number ('C9-4892,AF').

mimo_judge.reason: Both descriptions agree the page is a typed memorandum document with a 'RESTRICTED' stamp, but Description A adds a specific visual detail (hole-punch marks) not mentioned in B.

mimo_desc: A stamp in the bottom left corner reads "50 JAN 13 1954 GJ".
gpt_desc: *Image: Document scan of a newspaper clipping mounted on an archival page, showing the article headline “SAUCERS HELD SPACE SHIPS BY EX-MARINE” with subheadline “Hits AF Stand On Flying Disks,” arranged in three cut newspaper columns; the page includes handwritten annotations, black redaction marks, a “NOT RECORDED JAN 12 1954” stamp, a purple “JAN 13 1954” stamp, page/file markings, and a typed source checklist with “Times Herald” marked and date “12-28-53.”*
mini_desc: *Image: A document page with three vertically cut newspaper clipping columns about “flying saucers” and Air Force statements, plus a typed/printed distribution list at lower right labeled Times Herald, Wash. Post, Wash. News, Wash. Star, N. Y. Times, and N. Y. Compass with blank lines, handwritten blue annotations including “0-19” at top right, “62 83894-A” near the bottom center, and dates “NOT RECORDED 44 JAN 12 1954,” “12-28-53,” and “50 JAN 13 1954.”*

elements.description: Newspaper clipping with headline 'SAUCERS HELD SPACE SHIPS BY EX-MARINE' and subheadlines 'Hits AF Stand On Flying Disks'. Contains text columns and handwritten annotations such as 'Brought over 0-19', '62-83894-A', 'NOT RECORDED 44 JAN 12 1954', 'File S Ether', 'Times Herald P9', and 'Date: 12-28-53'.

mimo_judge.reason: The descriptions are about entirely different visual content: one describes a stamp, while the other describes a newspaper clipping illustration with specific headlines and annotations.

mimo_desc: hand-drawn sketch of the observed object from two viewpoints.
gpt_desc: *Image: Scanned typed document page marked “CONFIDENTIAL,” with five black punch holes along the top and two hand-drawn sketches near the lower half: a rounded irregular “Top” view labeled “traveling this way” with arrows, and a thin elongated “side view” labeled “traveling this way,” accompanied by typed annotation stating the objects seemed longer than wide and about 1/20th as thick as their width.*
mini_desc: *Image: A scanned typed letter page stamped “CONFIDENTIAL,” with two handwritten sketches at the bottom showing a round top view labeled “Top” and a thin side view labeled “side view,” each with arrows marked “traveling this way,” plus typed text including “Mirror Bright” and a note that the objects seemed longer than wide and about 1/20th their width.*

elements.description: A hand-drawn sketch depicting a round, circular object labeled 'Top' with an arrow indicating 'traveling this way' to the right. Below it, text states 'They seemed longer than wide, their thickness was about 1/20th of their width'.

A hand-drawn sketch depicting a thin, crescent-like or wing-shaped object labeled 'Side view' with an arrow indicating 'traveling this way' to the right. Below it, text reads 'Mirror Bright'. Further text states 'They did not appear to me to whirl or spin but seemed in fixed position, traveling as I have made drawing.'

mimo_judge.reason: Description A states the sketch is from two viewpoints but omits all specific details and quoted text (labels, arrows, captions) that Description B provides, creating a contradiction on the visual content's composition and detail.

mimo_desc: Blue ink stamp, oriented correctly:

INTERNAL SECURITY,” and dates/times such as “AUG 11 5 21 PM ’47” and “AUG 12 9 55 AM ’47.”*
mini_desc: *Image: A single scanned pink document page, mostly blank, with faint reversed typed text, two hole punches near the top edge, a torn upper-right corner, and multiple blue and black archival stamp impressions including “RECEIVED,” “RECEIVED TELETYPE UNIT,” dates/times, and “INTERNAL SECURITY F.B.I.”*

elements.description: A pinkish, aged paper with two hole-punch marks at the top and a torn corner at the top right. Faint, reversed typed text is visible from bleed-through on the reverse side. Several blue ink stamps are present: one circular stamp reading 'RECEIVED INTERNAL SECURITY F.B.I. AUG 12 9:55 AM'; one horizontal stamp reading 'RECEIVED TELETYPE UNIT F.B.I. DEPT. OF JUSTICE AUG 11 5:21 PM '47'; and another reading 'RECEIVED-LAUND F.B.I. DEPT. OF JUSTICE AUG 11 5:38 PM '47'. No distinct photographs, sketches, or diagrams are visible.

mimo_judge.reason: Description A mentions a single blue ink stamp, while Description B describes multiple stamps with specific, contradictory text (e.g., 'AUG 12' vs. 'AUG 11'), indicating a major factual inconsistency on the visual content.

mimo_desc: Handwritten signature in black ink over the date stamp: "Edw Mossburg"
gpt_desc: *Image: Scanned page of a typed “Office Memorandum” United States Government form dated 11/22/54, addressed to the Director, FBI, from SAC Cincinnati, concerning “Civilian Research, Interplanetary Flying Objects,” with handwritten annotations including “Flying Saucers,” file number “62-83894-350,” stamps, signatures, and red/blue pencil markings.*
mini_desc: *Image: Scanned FBI Office Memorandum form headed “United States Government,” dated 11/22/54, with typed text about “Civilian Research, Interplanetary Flying Objects” and numerous handwritten annotations, stamps, and file markings; no photograph or diagram.*

elements.description: Typed office memorandum from SAC, Cincinnati to Director, FBI, dated 11/22/54, regarding 'Civilian Research, Interplanetary Flying Objects'. Includes handwritten annotations ('Flying Saucers', '6/28', 'EHN/vd', red circled '8', '62-83894-350', 'P 9292', 'Investigative Div page 2', and illegible signatures) and several stamps ('Recorded - 24', 'Indexed - 24', '24 NOV 24 1954', '58 DEC 7 - 1954', and a purple stamp on the left). No photographs, sketches, diagrams, maps, or charts are present.

mimo_judge.reason: Description A claims a handwritten signature 'Edw Mossburg' is present over a date stamp, while Description B states 'No photographs, sketches, diagrams, maps, or charts are present' and lists different annotations, indicating a direct contradiction about the visual content of the signature.

mimo_desc: A manila file folder with a white paper slip stapled to its front, containing handwritten text in pink ink. The folder also has various stamps, handwritten notations, and file markings.
gpt_desc: *Image: Scan of a tan folder or document backing with a taped/slanted handwritten note in red-purple ink reading in part “HA! HA! WINCHELL HAS REVEALED THE ‘SAUCER’ SECRET. YOU CAN’T HIDE ANYTHING FROM US & OUR AGENTS LIKE WINCHELL COMMUNIST,” with additional marginal handwriting, two punched holes near the top, and archival stamps/notations including “RECORDED,” “37 JUL 15 1947,” “62-83894,” and page number “166.”*
mini_desc: *Image: A manila folder cover with two punch holes and handwritten/typed archival markings, including a centered white note card attached with a paper clip and covered in large handwritten text (“HA! HA! WINCHELL HAS REVEALED THE ‘SAUCER’ SECRET. YOU CAN’T HIDE ANYTHING FROM US & OUR AGENTS LIKE WINCHELL COMMUNIST”).*

elements.description: A faded, upside-down stamp in the upper left corner with text that is mostly illegible but appears to include the words 'COMMUNICATION' and 'I HOPE ATTACHED'.

A rectangular piece of light-colored paper attached to the main brown sheet. It contains a handwritten note in red ink that reads: 'HA! HA! WINCHELL HAS REVEALED THE "SAUCER" SECRET. YOU CANT HIDE ANYTHING FROM US & OUR AGENTS LIKE WINCHELL COMMUNIST'.

A stamp at the bottom right corner that reads 'RECORDED', with a handwritten file number '62-83894' above it, the letters 'FBI' below it, and a date stamp '37 JUL 16 1947'. Below this is the text 'EX-56'.

mimo_judge.reason: Description B provides extensive, specific quoted text from stamps and a handwritten note that Description A does not mention at all, creating a fundamental discrepancy in the visual content documented.

mimo_desc: Newspaper comic strip panel, part of a vertical column. In the top panel, a man in a cap and work clothes holds a shovel. Another man is partially visible in the background.
gpt_desc: *Image: Three vertically stacked newspaper comic panels: a partial “OUT OUR WAY” cartoon showing a man holding a hose or handle with speech balloons; a “SIDE GLANCES” cartoon showing a man outdoors holding a hose with caption text below and copyright “1946 BY NEA SERVICE, INC.”; and a partial “CARNIVAL” cartoon showing an interior scene with a seated person near a window and wall telephone.*
mini_desc: *Image: A scanned newspaper comic strip page with three vertically stacked black-and-white cartoon panels labeled “OUT OUR WAY,” “SIDE GLANCES,” and “CARNIVAL,” each showing a single cartoon scene with speech balloons and caption text.*

elements.description: A vertical newspaper comic strip clipping containing three distinct comic panels. The top panel is titled 'OUT OUR WAY' and depicts a man holding a mop or broom, with speech bubbles containing partially visible text. The middle panel is titled 'SIDE GLANCES' and depicts a man wearing an apron and holding a coiled rope, with a caption below that reads 'COPR. 1946 BY NEA SERVICE, INC.' The bottom panel is titled 'CARNIVAL' and depicts a man sitting in a room looking at a picture on the wall, with text above the panel that reads 'If you quit driving to work save enough money to buy Jun the yard!'.

mimo_judge.reason: Description A mentions a man with a shovel in one panel, while Description B describes three distinct panels with completely different content (mop/bro man, apron/rope man, man looking at picture) and specific quoted titles and text, indicating they are describing different visual content or one is fabricating details.

mimo_desc: A newspaper clipping pasted onto the page, headline reads "Swedish Pilot Reports 'Saucer'".
gpt_desc: *Image: Document scan of an FBI-style routing sheet containing one pasted newspaper clipping titled “Swedish Pilot Reports ‘Saucer’,” with handwritten notes including “Flying Saucers,” file number “162-83824-A,” date “Dec. 18, 1953,” routing/name checklists, and several date/record stamps including “JAN 6 1954” and “JAN 8 1954.”*
mini_desc: *Image: A scanned archive page with a pasted newspaper clipping titled “Swedish Pilot Reports ‘Saucer’,” handwritten blue notes including “Flying Saucers,” multiple stamped/inked file markings and dates, and a right-side checklist of names/publications with blank lines and one checked entry.*

elements.description: A yellowed newspaper clipping titled 'Swedish Pilot Reports Saucer' by United Press, dated Stockholm, Dec. 18. It contains a multi-paragraph text report about a saucer-shaped object sighting. There is no photograph or illustration within the clipping, only typed text.

mimo_judge.reason: Both sides identify the same core visual element—a newspaper clipping with the headline 'Swedish Pilot Reports Saucer'—and agree it contains only typed text with no photograph or illustration.

mimo_desc: A yellow manila document envelope or folder flap, oriented vertically. Two punched holes are visible at the top edge. A clear plastic fastener or label holder is affixed near the bottom center.

mimo_desc: In the background, visible as a faint, ghostly bleed-through from the opposite side of the page, is typewritten text. The text appears to be a formal memorandum or report. mimo_desc: Scanned document page. Top left shows the circular seal of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, Department of Justice. Below the seal is typed text: "In Reply, Please Refer to File No." followed by a blank space.
gpt_desc: *Image: Scanned typed FBI letterhead document page from the United States Department of Justice, Federal Bureau of Investigation, dated Detroit, Michigan, March 9, 1960, concerning “Unidentified Flying Object; Joseph Perry, Grand Blanc, Michigan – Complainant,” with an FBI seal at upper left partly obscured by a black mark and a red declassification stamp with handwritten notes at the bottom.*
mini_desc: *Image: A scanned typewritten FBI memorandum page with the FBI seal at upper left, headed “UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE / FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION,” dated “Detroit, Michigan March 9, 1960,” with a subject line about “Unidentified Flying Object; Joseph Perry, Grand Blanc, Michigan - Complainant,” and a pink stamped declassification mark with handwritten date and approval at the bottom right.*

elements.description: Back of a page with faint, illegible reversed text showing through. Two hole-punch marks are at the top. Two upside-down rubber stamps are at the bottom right: one reads 'REC'D BELMONT FBI - JUSTICE JAN 13 4 51 PM '55', and the other reads 'RECEIVED FBI COMMUNICATIONS JAN 13 4 15 PM '55'.

mimo_desc: A news clipping featuring a sketch of a disc-shaped flying saucer. The object is dome-shaped with a wide, flat rim. It has three visible rounded rectangular "windows" or ports around the dome, and a small, lattice-like antenna or spire protruding from the top center. The saucer is depicted resting on or hovering very close to a flat surface, casting a shadow. The sketch is rendered in a sepia-toned, newsprint style.
gpt_desc: *Image: Newspaper clipping mounted on a document page, showing an artist’s sketch of a domed “flying saucer” with a circular base, small windows, round portholes, a protruding antenna-like structure on top, and landing/support elements underneath; caption begins “THIS IS IT” and describes it as a sketch of a flying saucer allegedly seen by Herbert Long, with AP Wirephotos credit.*
mini_desc: *Image: A newspaper-style color illustration of a domed “flying saucer” with a small antenna on top, shown on a sandy background, with a caption block below beginning “THIS IS IT” and describing it as a sketch of the flying saucer.*

elements.description: A single hand-drawn sketch of a flying saucer depicted with a dome top, a diamond-shaped antenna, three visible D-shaped windows, and a wide base ring. Below the sketch is a caption reading: 'THIS IS IT A sketch of the flying saucer which Herbert Long, 29, a Kutztown, Pa., insurance salesman, contends he saw parked on a road 30 feet from his car. He said he was too frightened to approach it. He's shown (left, below) giving Leroy Gensler, artist, directions for the sketch. (AP Wirephotos)'.

mimo_judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content—a sketch of a disc-shaped flying saucer with a dome, windows, and antenna—but Description B adds specific caption text that Description A omits, and they use different terms for the antenna shape without a clear verbatim contradiction.

mimo_desc: Back of a yellowed, off-white document page. Two binder holes are visible at the top. The page is mostly blank, showing faint, reversed bleed-through text from the other side. At the bottom, there are two date stamps and a small piece of yellowed tape. One stamp reads "8 APR 26 1955". The other reads "APR 25  11 50 AM '65". Above the second stamp, faint text appears to read "ACCD-CCCP Y IURS". A very faint, reversed word "CONFIDENTIAL" is visible near the bottom left corner, and reversed text "SECRET RESTRICTED" and "EYES ONLY" is visible near the bottom right corner. A small, rectangular piece of yellowed tape with two horizontal slits is affixed to the right margin near the bottom.
gpt_desc: *Image: Back side of a pale document page with two round punched holes near the top edge, faint bleed-through text, an attached small blank slip with four handwritten parenthesis-like marks on the right side, and inverted date stamps near the bottom reading “APR 26 1955” and “APR 26 11 50 AM ’55.”*
mini_desc: *Image: A blank aged document page with two black binder punch holes near the top edge, a small yellow sticky-note-like tab at lower right, and faint mirrored/stamped text near the bottom including “APR 26 1965” and “APR 25 11 50 AM 65.”*

elements.description: A mostly blank, aged paper page showing the reverse side of a document with faint, reversed bleed-through text from the front side (including partially legible reversed words). There are two punch holes at the top edge, two small red stamps at the bottom left reading '8 APR 26 1955', a blue date stamp at the bottom right reading 'APR 25 11 50 AM '65', and a small yellow sticky note attached to the right edge.

elements.description: A blue digital declassification stamp in the top left corner with the text 'DECLASSIFIED', 'Authority:', and 'NND 917033'.
A small blue digital label at the bottom center containing the text 'Best Possible Image'.

mimo_judge.reason: Description A claims multiple specific visual elements (SECRET stamps, handwritten notes, a taped index card, a date stamp, a tab label, and handwritten numbers) that Description B does not mention at all, indicating a major discrepancy in the perceived visual content of the page.
